description: Deterministic conflict-resolution policy for the Vecells blueprint corpus. Higher-ranked tiers win when statements disagree, except that forensic findings may add mandatory patch rows and anti-regression guardrails but may not weaken higher-tier canonical contracts.

tiers.conflict_rule: This tier is the canonical foundation for object shape, invariants, lifecycle control, route intent, release freeze, and assurance trust.
Relevant phase files refine workflow behavior for their own phase but must remain compatible with Phase 0 invariants and cross-cutting shell/runtime law.
Cross-cutting blueprints define canonical UI, runtime, admin, support, and specialist operating law whenever a summary layer is broader than the concrete contract.
Phase cards align summary language and sequencing but must not override Phase 0, relevant phase files, or specialized blueprints.

wins_on: baseline continuity and control-plane tuple visibility
tiers.conflict_rule: The audited flow is a supporting baseline. If it diverges from Phase 0 or a relevant phase blueprint, the canonical blueprint wins and the flow must be corrected.
Forensic findings must be represented as patch requirements, exclusions, or anti-regression rules; they tighten the corpus but do not authorize weaker semantics.
The bootstrap summary orients later work but never overrides canonical or specialized sources.
Supporting design references help interpret the front-end direction but do not override explicit shell, accessibility, runtime, or phase contracts.

global_rules.statement: Preserve the orthogonality of submissionEnvelopeState, workflowState, safetyState, and identityState.
LifecycleCoordinator is the sole cross-domain authority for request closure and governed reopen decisions.
Web, NHS App jump-off, telephony, secure-link continuation, and support-assisted capture are one governed intake lineage, not separate back-office workflows.
Supplier-specific behavior belongs behind adapter capability contracts rather than domain-local business logic.
Degraded, fallback, repair, and ambiguity states are first-class outputs and must not be flattened into optimistic milestone prose.
